advantages: Catches bugs early (a second pair of eyes finds what tests miss).
Spreads knowledge across the team (reviewers learn the codebase, authors learn from feedback).
Creates an audit trail (who approved what change, and why).
Enforces standards (branch protection turns suggestions into requirements).
Acts as a discussion forum tied to the actual diff, not Slack ephemera.
disadvantages: Adds latency — every change waits on a human.
Tempts bike-shedding (long debates about preferences disguised as technical points).
Can become rubber-stamping when reviewers are overloaded or under-incentivized.
Over-strict policy (3 approvals, slow CI) compounds latency for tiny changes.
